# integrating SIR-type model at the end to predict total carbon emissions
# displaying notebook on nbviewer/mybinder
# model based on soil type, clay and soil carbon

# note: At the global scale, there is consensus that Q10 decreases from polar to temperate to tropical regions (Bekku et al., 2003; Zhou et al., 2009a) and that it is inversely correlated with mean annual temperature (Peng et al., 2009; Zheng et al., 2009).

import zipfile
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import xarray as xr
from scipy.optimize import minimize
from scipy.spatial import KDTree
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import holoviews as hv
import geoviews as gv
hv.extension("bokeh")
hv.output(widget_location='bottom')
gv.extension("bokeh")
from bokeh.models import HoverTool
import hvplot.xarray
import hvplot.pandas
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score,  mean_absolute_error
from modsim import *

hover_mrsos = HoverTool(tooltips=[
    ("Site Name", "@{Site_name}"),
    ('Biome', '@Biome'),
    ('Annual soil respiration rate in g CO2 m-2', '@Rs_annual'),
    ('Soil Moisture in kg m-3', '@mrsos')
])

hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[
    ("Site Name", "@{Site_name}"),
    ("Biome", '@Biome'),

def select_scenario(scenario):
    if scenario == "ssp1_2_6":
        return temperature_datasets[0], datasets[0]
    elif scenario == "ssp2_4_5":
        return temperature_datasets[1], datasets[1]
    elif scenario == "ssp5_8_5":
        return temperature_datasets[2], datasets[2]
    else:
        raise ValueError('Invalid scenario. Please choose from "ssp1_2_6", "ssp2_4_5", or "ssp5_8_5".')
    
def get_location(i):
    query_point = [forests["Latitude"].iloc[i], forests["Longitude"].iloc[i]]
    return query_point

#function that models soil respiration to the SRDB data as an exponential function of temperature and attributes a contribution to soil moisture
def Rs_TM_function(params, X_packed, model_M = True):               
    """T = temperature, M = moisture"""
    T = X_packed[:,0]
    M = X_packed[:,1]                    
    R_base, Q10, T_base, alpha, beta, gamma, delta = params
    if model_M == True:
        return (R_base * (Q10 ** ((T - T_base) / 10))) + (((alpha*M) + (beta*(M**2)) + gamma)) - delta*(T*M)
    else:
        return R_base * (Q10 ** ((T - T_base) / 10))

def Rs_T_loss_function(params, X_packed, y_true):
    y_pred = Rs_TM_function(params, X_packed, model_M = False)
    return np.sum((y_true - y_pred)**2)                 # return mean-squared-error

def Rs_TM_loss_function(params, X_packed, y_true):
    y_pred = Rs_TM_function(params, X_packed)
    return np.sum((y_true - y_pred)**2)                 # return mean-squared-error

def plot_3d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid, title, indices, test_indices):
    fig = go.Figure()

    # add a surface plot for the model
    fig.add_trace(go.Surface(
    x=temp_grid,
    y=moist_grid,
    z=y_grid,
    colorscale='RdYlGn_r',
    opacity=0.7,
    name=title,
    showscale=True,
    colorbar=dict(
    title="Rs (g CO2/m²/yr)"
    )))

    for i in biomes:     
            train_data = forests.iloc[indices][forests.iloc[indices]["Biome"] == i]
            test_data = forests.iloc[test_indices][forests.iloc[test_indices]["Biome"] == i]
            
            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter3d(
            x=train_data["MAT"].values,
            y=train_data["mrsos"].values,
            z=train_data["Rs_annual"].values,
            mode='markers',
            marker=dict(size=4, opacity=0.3, 
                            color=train_data["Rs_annual"].values,  # Color by z-value
                            colorscale='RdYlGn_r',
                            showscale = False),
            name=f'Training data – {i} biomes'
            ))

            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter3d(
            x=test_data["MAT"].values,
            y=test_data["mrsos"].values,
            z=test_data["Rs_annual"].values,
            mode='markers',
            marker=dict(size=4, symbol = "x", opacity=0.3, 
                            color=test_data["Rs_annual"].values,  # Color by z-value
                            colorscale='RdYlGn_r',
                            showscale = False),
            name=f'Testing data – {i} biomes'
            ))

    fig.update_layout(
    title=title,
    scene=dict(
            xaxis_title='Temperature (°C)',
            yaxis_title='Soil Moisture (kg/m²)',
            zaxis_title='Soil Respiration Rs (g CO2/m²/year)'
    ),
    width=1000,
    height=900,
    legend=dict(x=-0.25,
                    y=0.98,
                    xanchor='left',
                    yanchor='top'
                    ))

    fig.show()

def plot_2d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test, title):
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
    #Contour plot with X_grid modeled on respective model
    contour = plt.contourf(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid, levels=25, cmap='RdYlGn_r')
    plt.colorbar(contour, label='Predicted Respiration (g CO2/m²/year)')

    # Overlay data
    plt.scatter(X_train[:, 0], X_train[:, 1], c=y_train, 
                edgecolors='black', s=50, cmap='RdYlGn_r', alpha=0.7, label='Train')
    plt.scatter(X_test[:, 0], X_test[:, 1], c=y_test, 
                edgecolors='red', marker='x', s=100, cmap='RdYlGn_r', label='Test')

    plt.xlabel('Temperature (°C)', fontsize=12)
    plt.ylabel('Soil Moisture (kg/m²)', fontsize=12)
    plt.title(title, fontsize=14)
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(alpha=0.3)
    plt.show()
In [55]:
def run_simulation(kfold_splits = 10, model_M = True, RF = False): #by_biome = False
        X = forests[["MAT", "mrsos"]].values
        y = forests["Rs_annual"].values
        R2_values = []
        best_r2_score = -np.inf
        # Create prediction grid for the visualizations
        temp_range = np.linspace(X[:, 0].min(), X[:, 0].max(), 100)
        moist_range = np.linspace(X[:, 1].min(), X[:, 1].max(), 100)
        temp_grid, moist_grid = np.meshgrid(temp_range, moist_range)
        X_grid = np.c_[temp_grid.ravel(), moist_grid.ravel()]

        skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits= kfold_splits, shuffle=True, random_state=42) # keeping random_state constant for reproducible results
        i=0

        if RF == False:
                for train_x_values, test_x_values in skf.split(X, forests["Biome"]):
                        i += 1
                        X_train, X_test = X[train_x_values], X[test_x_values]
                        y_train, y_test = y[train_x_values], y[test_x_values]

                        if model_M == True:
                                print("Running temperature and moisture model...")
                                results = minimize(Rs_TM_loss_function, 
                                        x0=[800, 2, 10, 25, 1, 1, 1],
                                        args=(X_train, y_train),
                                        method='L-BFGS-B', # L-BFGS-B, Nelder-Mead
                                        bounds=[(0, 10000), (0.1, 20), (-10, 30), (0, 50), (-1, 0), (0, 1000), (-100, 100)])      # set lower and upper bounds
                                fitted_params = results.x
                                #print(f"Fitted the following parameters for run {i}:", fitted_params)

                        else:
                                print("Running temperature model...")
                                results = minimize(Rs_T_loss_function, 
                                        x0=[800, 2, 10, 1, 1, 1, 1],
                                        args=(X_train, y_train),
                                        method='L-BFGS-B', # L-BFGS-B, Nelder-Mead
                                        bounds=[(0, 10000), (0.1, 20), (-10, 30), (-100, 100), (-100, 100), (-100, 100), (-100, 100)])      # set lower and upper bounds
                                fitted_params = results.x
                                #print(f"Fitted the following parameters for run {i}:", fitted_params)

                        #validation with mean absolute error and R squared
                        y_pred = Rs_TM_function(fitted_params, np.sort(X_test), model_M)
                        R2_values.append(r2_score(y_test, y_pred))
                        print(f"Run {i}: Mean absolute error is {mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_pred):.1f}, R² is {r2_score(y_test, y_pred):.3f}")

                        if r2_score(y_test, y_pred) > best_r2_score:
                                best_r2_score = r2_score(y_test, y_pred)
                                best_params = fitted_params
                                indices = train_x_values
                                test_indices = test_x_values

                print(f"\n The maximum R² value in this run was {best_r2_score:.3f} \n")
                if model_M == False:
                        print(f"The parameters for that run were: \n base respiration rate: {best_params[0]:3f}, \n base Q10 value {best_params[1]:3f}, \n base temperature {best_params[2]:3f}, \n")
                
                else:
                        print(f"The parameters for that run were: \n base respiration rate: {best_params[0]:3f}, \n base Q10 value {best_params[1]:3f}, \n base temperature {best_params[2]:3f}, \n alpha, beta, gamma, delta: {best_params[3]:3f}, {best_params[4]:3f}, {best_params[5]:3f}, {best_params[6]:3f} ")
                
                print(f"\n The average R² value was: {np.mean(R2_values):.3f}")
                
                y_grid_TM_model = Rs_TM_function(best_params, X_grid, model_M).reshape(temp_grid.shape)
                
                if model_M == True:
                        # print one representative interactive 3D plot for this model splitting data by biome
                        plot_3d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid_TM_model, "Temperature Moisture Model Predictions", indices, test_indices)
                        plot_2d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid_TM_model, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test, title= 'Temperature and Moisture Model Prediction Landscape')
                else:
                        plot_3d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid_TM_model, "Temperature Model Predictions", indices, test_indices)
                        plot_2d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid_TM_model, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test, title= 'Temperature Model Prediction Landscape')
                
                return best_params

        elif RF == True: 
                print("Running Random Forests regressor...")
                for train_x_values, test_x_values in skf.split(X, forests["Biome"]):
                        i += 1
                        X_train, X_test = X[train_x_values], X[test_x_values]
                        y_train, y_test = y[train_x_values], y[test_x_values]

                        rf = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=100, random_state=42)
                        rf.fit(X_train, y_train)

                        y_pred = rf.predict(X_test)
                        R2_values.append(r2_score(y_test, y_pred))
                        print(f"Random forest regressor R² for run {i}: {r2_score(y_test, y_pred):.3f}, mean absolute error (MAE) for run {i}: {mean_absolute_error(y_test, y_pred):.1f}")
                        if r2_score(y_test, y_pred) > best_r2_score:
                                best_r2_score = r2_score(y_test, y_pred)
                                indices = train_x_values
                                test_indices = test_x_values
                                y_grid = rf.predict(X_grid).reshape(temp_grid.shape)
                        
                print("\n The following was the maximum R² value in this run: ",best_r2_score)
                print("The average R² value was: ", np.mean(R2_values))
                
                # print one representative interactive 3D plot for this model splitting data by biome
                plot_3d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid, "Random Forests Regression Model Predictions", indices, test_indices)
                plot_2d_results(temp_grid, moist_grid, y_grid, X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test, title= 'Random Forest Regressor Prediction Landscape')

# If you require the Climate Data Store datasets, please refer to the fetching_CDS_data.ipynb file
# Note that you have to make an account and set up your own API Access key

historical_moisture_datasets = []

datasets = []

temperature_datasets = []

def open_nc_from_zip(zip_file_path, datasets_list):
    # Open the zip file
    for zip_data in zip_file_path:
        with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_data, "r") as zip_ref:
            # List all the file names in the zip archive
            file_names = zip_ref.namelist()

            for file_name in file_names:
            # Open the nc file in the zip archive
                if ".nc" in file_name:
                    print(f"Data file in the zip archive: {file_name}")
                    
                    # Read directly from zip
                    with zip_ref.open(file_name) as file:
                        dataset = xr.open_dataset(file, engine="h5netcdf").load()  # or engine="scipy"
                        #df = dataset.to_dataframe().reset_index()
                        datasets_list.append(dataset)
                    print(f"Processed file: {file_name}")

    print("Files processed")

# Open the zip file and extract data as historical_moisture_dataset
# Replace lists with your actual zip file path
open_nc_from_zip(["historical_data_moisture.zip"], historical_moisture_datasets)
historical_moisture_dataset = historical_moisture_datasets[0]

# Open the zip files and extract datasets for SSP predictions of moisture and temperature
open_nc_from_zip(["ssp126_data.zip", "ssp245_data.zip", "ssp585_data.zip"], datasets)

open_nc_from_zip(["ssp126_data_temperature.zip", "ssp245_data_temperature.zip", "ssp585_data_temperature.zip"], temperature_datasets)

# print(datasets[2].info()) # for checking the structure of the dataset

Learn more about SSP scenarios: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shared_Socioeconomic_Pathways

scenario = input("Enter the warming scenario you want to model. Please choose either ssp1_2_6, ssp2_4_5, or ssp5_8_5. \n These correspond to 1.8 °C, 2.7 °C, and 4.4 °C warming by 2100.")

temperature_dataset = select_scenario(scenario)[0]
moisture_dataset = select_scenario(scenario)[1]

print(f"Confirming scenario {scenario}. \n")

min_val_temp = temperature_dataset["tas"].min().values
max_val_temp = temperature_dataset["tas"].max().values
min_val_m = moisture_dataset["mrsos"].min().values
max_val_m = moisture_dataset["mrsos"].max().values
min_val_hm = historical_moisture_dataset["mrsos"].min().values
max_val_hm = historical_moisture_dataset["mrsos"].max().values

# plotting the data from the Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) using hvplot
temp = temperature_dataset["tas"].hvplot(x="lon",
                                    y="lat",
                                    groupby="time",
                                    cmap="RdBu_r", 
                                    frame_width=600,
                                    frame_height=400, 
                                    clim=(min_val_temp, max_val_temp), 
                                    hover = False)

moisture = moisture_dataset["mrsos"].hvplot(x="lon",
                                        y="lat",
                                        groupby="time",
                                        cmap="YlGnBu",
                                        frame_width=600,
                                        frame_height=400,
                                        clim=(min_val_m, max_val_m),
                                        alpha=0.7,
                                        hover = False)                              # Adjust alpha for better visibility

historical_moisture = historical_moisture_dataset["mrsos"].hvplot(x="lon",
                                        y="lat",
                                        groupby="time",
                                        cmap="YlGnBu",
                                        frame_width=600,
                                        frame_height=400,
                                        clim=(min_val_hm, max_val_hm),
                                        alpha=0.7)

temp

#getting the data from the SoilRespirationDataBase (SRDB) and storing as a pandas dataframe
Rs_data = pd.read_csv("srdb-20250503a/srdb-data.csv")
equations_data = pd.read_csv("srdb-20250503a/srdb-equations.csv")
#study_data = pd.read_csv("srdb-20250503a/srdb-studies.csv")

# Uncomment the merge if you want to include ,"Q10_0_10", "Q10_5_15", "Q10_10_20", "Q10_0_20", "Q10_other1", "Q10_other1_temp_min", "Q10_other1_temp_max"
df = Rs_data#.merge(equations_data, on="Record_number", how="inner") 
df.loc[df["Longitude"] < 0, "Longitude"] += 360
df = df.dropna(subset=["Latitude", "Longitude", "Biome", "Ecosystem_type", "Rs_annual", "MAT"])         # for dropping rows with missing values in the specified columns

#selecting only the columns relevant for modeling
df = df[["Record_number", "Study_number", "Site_name", "Latitude", "Longitude", "Biome", "Ecosystem_type", "Rs_annual", "Study_midyear","MAT"]]                 
df = df[(df['Rs_annual'] > 0) & (df['Rs_annual'] < 5000)]
df = df[(df['Study_midyear'] < 2015)]                                                                   # excluding data outside of the range of historical moisture dataset

biomes = ["Boreal", "Temperate", "Tropical", "Subtropical"]                                             #list biomes of interest for study
forests = df[df["Biome"].isin(biomes) & df["Ecosystem_type"].isin(["Forest"])]
#forests.dtypes

forests['Site_name'] = forests['Site_name'].astype(str).fillna('Unknown')
radius_scaling = 3
forests["r"] = forests["Rs_annual"]/radius_scaling                                                                             # for calculating the radius of the circles in the map based on the annual soil respiration rates

# overlaying the data from the SRDB on a map using hvplot, geopandas, and geoviews
plot = forests.hvplot.points(
          x='Longitude',
          y='Latitude', 
          c='Biome',               # set color by biome type
          cmap='Dark2',        # select colormap (viridis, magma, inferno, etc.)
          size='r',                # set size by magnitude of soil respiration
          alpha=0.3,               # Adjust point transparency
          legend='left',           # choose from ['top_right', 'top_left', 'bottom_left', 'bottom_right', 'right', 'left', 'top', 'bottom']
          frame_width=600,
          frame_height=400,
          hover_cols=['Rs_annual', 'Biome', "Site_name"]  # Specify popup fields
     )

Rs_plot = forests.hvplot.points(
          x='Longitude',
          y='Latitude', 
          c='Rs_annual',           # set color by magnitude of annual soil respiration
          cmap='magma_r',
          size='r',                # set size by magnitude of soil respiration

          frame_width=600,
          frame_height=400,
          hover_cols=['Rs_annual', 'Biome', "Site_name"]  # Specify popup fields
     ).opts(tools=[hover, 'pan', 'wheel_zoom', 'reset'])

In [33]:
#Locate nearest neighbors to the SRDB entries on the latitude and longitude grid of CDS data for each individual data point

lat_array = (temperature_dataset["lat"]).values
lon_array = (temperature_dataset["lon"]).values

lat_grid, lon_grid = np.meshgrid(lat_array, lon_array)
grid = np.column_stack([lat_grid.ravel(), lon_grid.ravel()])               # Flatten the meshgrid and stack coordinates for KDTree

tree = KDTree(grid)                                                        # Instantiate K Nearest Neighbor search

lat_nn = []
lon_nn = []
biome_nn = []
record_nn = []
year_nn = []

for i in range(len(forests["Latitude"])):
    #distance, index = tree.query(get_location(i))
    # Add biome, record number, latitude and longitude values to their respective lists
    biome_nn.append(forests["Biome"].iloc[i])
    biome_nn.append(forests["Biome"].iloc[i])
    record_nn.append(forests["Record_number"].iloc[i].astype(int))
    record_nn.append(forests["Record_number"].iloc[i].astype(int))
    year_nn.append(forests["Study_midyear"].iloc[i].astype(int))
    year_nn.append(forests["Study_midyear"].iloc[i].astype(int))
    lat_nn.append(grid[tree.query(get_location(i))[1]][0])                                                        
    lon_nn.append(grid[tree.query(get_location(i))[1]][1])

# select respective times and temperatures for the grid locations
points = temperature_dataset.sel(lat=xr.DataArray(lat_nn), lon=xr.DataArray(lon_nn), method='nearest')            
temp_df = points.to_dataframe().reset_index()[["time", "tas", "lat", "lon"]]

temp_df["tas"] = temp_df["tas"] - 273.15                                   # converting from Kelvin to degrees Celsius
temp_df["Biome"] = biome_nn * len(temp_df["time"].unique())                # adding biome information to the dataframe
temp_df["Record_number"] = record_nn * len(temp_df["time"].unique())       # add record number column
temp_df["year"] = pd.to_datetime(temp_df['time'].astype(str)).dt.year      # create year column
temp_df = temp_df[::2].reset_index(drop=True)                              # rows are duplicate, remove superfluous data    

# repeat the same process with the projected times and moisture values for the grid locations
moisture_points = moisture_dataset.sel(lat=xr.DataArray(lat_nn), lon=xr.DataArray(lon_nn), method='nearest')
moisture_df = moisture_points.to_dataframe().reset_index()[["time", "mrsos", "lat", "lon"]]
moisture_df["Record_number"] = record_nn * len(moisture_df["time"].unique())
moisture_df = moisture_df[::2].reset_index(drop=True)    

projections_df = pd.concat([temp_df, moisture_df[['mrsos']]], axis=1)

# repeat the same process with the projected times and historical moisture values for the grid locations
hist_moisture_points = historical_moisture_dataset.sel(lat=xr.DataArray(lat_nn), lon=xr.DataArray(lon_nn), method='nearest')
hist_moisture_df = hist_moisture_points.to_dataframe().reset_index()[["time", "mrsos", "lat", "lon"]]
hist_moisture_df["Record_number"] = record_nn * len(hist_moisture_df["time"].unique())
hist_moisture_df["year"] = pd.to_datetime(hist_moisture_df['time'].astype(str)).dt.year
hist_moisture_df["study_year"] = year_nn * len(hist_moisture_df["time"].unique())
hist_moisture_df = hist_moisture_df[hist_moisture_df['year'] == hist_moisture_df['study_year']] # only keep the historical moisture data for the middle year of the study
hist_moisture_df = hist_moisture_df[["mrsos", "lat", "lon", "year", "Record_number"]]
hist_moisture_df = hist_moisture_df.groupby(["lat", "lon", "Record_number","year"])['mrsos'].mean().reset_index().dropna()

forests = forests.merge(hist_moisture_df, on="Record_number", how="inner")
In [34]:
hist_moist_plot = forests.hvplot.points(
    x='Longitude',
    y='Latitude', 
    c='mrsos',   
    alpha=0.6,
    size = "r",                
    legend='left',           
    frame_width=600,
    frame_height=400,
    hover_cols=['Rs_annual', 'Biome', "mrsos", "Site_name"]
).opts(tools=[hover_mrsos, 'pan', 'wheel_zoom', 'reset'])

nnplot = forests.hvplot.points(
     x='lon',
     y='lat', 
     c='white',
     size=20,     
     alpha=0.4,                 
     legend='left',        
     frame_width=600,
     frame_height=400,
     hover = False
)

# Checking visually how well the fit for the nearest neighbors on the grid works
hist_moist_plot * nnplot

best_params = run_simulation(kfold_splits = 10, model_M = model_M_global, RF = False)

# modeling soil respiration using the data from the CDS and SRDB, and plotting the results using hvplot
# selecting nearest neighbor temperature from Temperature dataset

projections_df["Rs_pred"] = (Rs_TM_function(best_params, projections_df[["tas", "mrsos"]].values, model_M_global))/12      # generate soil repspiration predictions based on nearest neighbor temperature, moisture, and model parameters

annual_predictions = projections_df.groupby(["lat", "lon", "Record_number","year", "Biome"])[['tas', "mrsos"]].mean().reset_index()
annual_predictions["Rs_annual_predictions"] = (Rs_TM_function(best_params, annual_predictions[["tas", "mrsos"]].values, model_M_global))

biome_predictions = annual_predictions.groupby(["year", "Biome"])[["Rs_annual_predictions"]].mean().reset_index()

for i in biomes:
    biome = biome_predictions[biome_predictions["Biome"] == i]
    plt.plot(biome["year"], biome["Rs_annual_predictions"], alpha= 0.6, label =f"{i}")

plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Soil Respiration (g CO2/m²/year)')
plt.legend()
plt.show()

annual_predictions["r"] = annual_predictions["Rs_annual_predictions"]  / radius_scaling

                        y='lat',

In [61]:
#modeling soil respiration using the data from the CDS and SRDB, and plotting the results using matplotlib

tc = 2           # time between contacts in days 
tr = 5             # recovery time in days

beta = 1 / tc      # contact rate in per day
gamma = 1 / tr     # recovery rate in per day

# 4 billion hectares forest
# tropical 45
# subtropical 11
# temperate 16
# boreal 27

# srdb averages for:
# crops
# grazing
# wild grassland

def make_system(beta, gamma, absolute=True, t_end=75):
    init = State(Plants=560, Atmosphere=750, Soils=1500, Fossil_Fuels=10000, Earth_Crust=100000000, Surface_Ocean=725, Deep_and_Abyssal_Ocean=37275)
    if absolute == False:
        init /= init.sum()

    return System(init=init, t_end=t_end,
                  beta=beta, gamma=gamma)

system = make_system(beta, gamma)
show(system)

def update_func(t, state, system):
    Plants, Atmosphere, Soils, Fossil_Fuels, Earth_Crust, Surface_Ocean, Deep_and_Abyssal_Ocean = state.Plants, state.Atmosphere, state.Soils, state.Fossil_Fuels, state.Earth_Crust, state.Surface_Ocean, state.Deep_and_Abyssal_Ocean

    Litterfall = 59
    Photosynthesis = 120
    Plant_Respiration = 59
    Volcanic_Eruptions = 0.1
    Burning_Fossil_Fuels = 7.7
    Soil_Respiration = 58
    Rivers = 0.9
    Burial_to_Sediments = 0.1
    Deforestation = 1.1
    Ocean_Uptake = 92
    Ocean_Loss = 90
    
    Plants -= Litterfall
    Soils += Litterfall

    Plants += Photosynthesis
    Atmosphere -= Photosynthesis

    Plants -= Plant_Respiration
    Atmosphere += Plant_Respiration

    Earth_Crust -= Volcanic_Eruptions
    Atmosphere += Volcanic_Eruptions

    Fossil_Fuels -= Burning_Fossil_Fuels
    Atmosphere += Burning_Fossil_Fuels

    Soils -= Soil_Respiration
    Atmosphere += Soil_Respiration

    Soils -= Rivers
    Surface_Ocean += Rivers
    Surface_Ocean -= Burial_to_Sediments
    Earth_Crust += Burial_to_Sediments

    Plants -= Deforestation
    Atmosphere += Deforestation

    Atmosphere -= Ocean_Uptake
    Surface_Ocean += Ocean_Uptake

    Surface_Ocean -= Ocean_Loss
    Atmosphere += Ocean_Loss
    
    return State(Plants=Plants, Atmosphere=Atmosphere, Soils=Soils, Fossil_Fuels=Fossil_Fuels, Earth_Crust=Earth_Crust, Surface_Ocean=Surface_Ocean, Deep_and_Abyssal_Ocean=Deep_and_Abyssal_Ocean)

state = update_func(0, system.init, system)
show(state)


def run_carbon_simulation(system, update_func):
    frame = TimeFrame(columns=system.init.index)
    frame.loc[0] = system.init
    
    for t in range(0, system.t_end):
        frame.loc[t+1] = update_func(t, frame.loc[t], system)
    
    return frame

results = run_carbon_simulation(system, update_func)
#print(results)

def plot_results(Plants, Atmosphere, Soils, Surface_Ocean):
    Plants.plot(style='--', label='Plants')
    Atmosphere.plot(style='-', label='Atmosphere')
    Soils.plot(style=':', label='Soils')
    Surface_Ocean.plot(style='--', label='Surface Ocean')
    decorate(xlabel='Time (years)',
                ylabel='Amount of Carbon (GtC)')
             
plot_results(results.Plants, results.Atmosphere, results.Soils, results.Surface_Ocean)
